clue_ferrie_oswald_cap.title: Ferrie was Oswald's Civil Air Patrol captain
clue_ferrie_oswald_cap.description: David Ferrie was Lee Harvey Oswald's Civil Air Patrol captain in New Orleans when Oswald was 15-16. Photographs exist showing them together. Ferrie denied knowing Oswald — but the photos prove otherwise. This links the alleged assassin to a CIA operative and Mafia associate years before Dallas.

clue_ferrie_houston_trip.title: Ferrie's 350-mile drive on assassination night
clue_ferrie_houston_trip.description: On the night of November 22, David Ferrie drove 350 miles through a thunderstorm to a Houston ice skating rink. He claims it was recreational. The trip appears to be either alibi construction or escape route preparation. Ferrie was connected to Oswald, Banister, and Marcello.

clue_ferrie_death.title: Ferrie died the same day his associate was axe-murdered
clue_ferrie_death.description: David Ferrie died February 22, 1967 — days after being named in Garrison's investigation. Ruled natural causes, but two suicide notes were found. On the SAME day, his associate Eladio del Valle was murdered in Miami — shot in the heart and struck with an axe. Two connected men dead on the same day.

clue_banister_544.title: Banister's secretary saw Oswald in the office
clue_banister_544.description: Guy Banister's secretary Delphine Roberts confirmed she saw Oswald in Banister's office on multiple occasions. A supposed communist operating from the office of a violently anti-communist former FBI agent. This suggests Oswald's pro-Castro activities were a manufactured intelligence cover.

clue_banister_cia_ops.title: The CIA-Mafia-Cuban exile triangle
clue_banister_cia_ops.description: Banister ran anti-Castro operations with CIA backing, was connected to Marcello's organization, and used Cuban exile militants. His 544 Camp Street office was the nexus where CIA, Mafia, and Cuban exile elements converged — the three forces most widely suspected of conspiring against Kennedy.

clue_marcello_motive.title: Marcello's 'cut off the head' strategy
clue_marcello_motive.description: Marcello had the strongest motive: RFK had him kidnapped and deported. His strategy was elegant — killing RFK would intensify prosecution, but killing JFK would remove RFK because LBJ loathed Bobby. Marcello told associates: 'If you cut off a dog's tail, the dog will only keep biting. But if you cut off its head, the dog will die.'

clue_ruby_mob_network.title: Ruby connected Marcello to Oswald's silence
clue_ruby_mob_network.description: Ruby's phone records link him to Marcello's organization (Pecora), Hoffa (Baker), and Trafficante (McWillie). Ruby silenced Oswald before he could talk. David Ferrie worked for Marcello's lawyer. The HSCA concluded the mob had 'the motive, means, and opportunity' and that Ruby's act 'was not spontaneous.'

clue_marcello_confession.title: Marcello allegedly confessed in prison
clue_marcello_confession.description: FBI informant Jack Van Laningham reported that Marcello, in prison in the 1980s, admitted involvement in the Kennedy assassination — saying he set up Oswald and arranged for Ruby to silence him. On November 22, 1963, Marcello was in federal court (the perfect alibi) and was acquitted that same afternoon.

clue_garrison_shaw_cia.title: Clay Shaw WAS a CIA contact — confirmed decades later
clue_garrison_shaw_cia.description: Garrison prosecuted Clay Shaw for conspiracy in 1969. Shaw was acquitted. But CIA documents released decades later confirmed Shaw was a CIA domestic contact — exactly as Garrison alleged. The CIA's HSCA liaison, George Joannides, was actually a material witness running operations in Miami in 1963.

clue_garrison_544_nexus.title: Garrison identified the 544 Camp Street conspiracy nexus
clue_garrison_544_nexus.description: Garrison was first to publicly connect 544 Camp Street: Oswald's FPCC leaflets, Banister's anti-Castro operations, Ferrie's CIA and Mafia connections — all in the same building. This triangle of intelligence, organized crime, and Cuban exile elements is the most widely accepted conspiracy framework.

clue_garrison_zapruder.title: Garrison first showed the Zapruder film publicly
clue_garrison_zapruder.description: Jim Garrison was the first to show the Zapruder film publicly, projecting it in the Shaw trial courtroom in 1969. Until then, the American public had never seen the actual assassination footage. Life magazine had kept the film locked away. When jurors saw the head snap 'back and to the left,' audible gasps filled the room.

clue_bolden_chicago_plot.title: The Chicago plot — a mirror of Dallas
clue_bolden_chicago_plot.description: In early November 1963, the Secret Service uncovered a plot to assassinate JFK during a Chicago motorcade. Sniper Thomas Arthur Vallee — an ex-Marine with anti-Castro connections — was arrested. The plot mirrored Dallas exactly. The motorcade was canceled. The Warren Commission was never told.

clue_bolden_silenced.title: Secret Service agent imprisoned for trying to testify
clue_bolden_silenced.description: When Abraham Bolden tried to inform the Warren Commission about the Chicago plot, he was arrested on fabricated charges. The key witness recanted, saying prosecutors pressured him to lie. Bolden served six years in prison. He was pardoned by President Biden in 2022.

clue_wade_corrected.title: Ruby corrected the DA at the midnight press conference
clue_wade_corrected.description: DA Wade misidentified Oswald's organization as the 'Free Cuba Committee.' Jack Ruby corrected him: 'Fair Play for Cuba Committee.' A nightclub owner displaying detailed knowledge of the suspect's political affiliations — at midnight, hours after the assassination. How did Ruby know?

clue_wade_chaos.title: Dallas Police HQ: a security catastrophe
clue_wade_chaos.description: Over 100 reporters, cameras, and unauthorized civilians packed the third floor of police HQ. No security screening. Anyone could walk in — and Jack Ruby did, repeatedly. This atmosphere of chaos made Oswald's murder possible. Was the lack of security incompetence or design?

clue_hosty_surveillance.title: FBI agent destroyed Oswald's note
clue_hosty_surveillance.description: FBI Agent James Hosty visited Marina before the assassination — the FBI had Oswald on their radar. Days before Dallas, Oswald hand-delivered a note to the FBI office. After the assassination, Hosty destroyed the note on orders from his superior. Its contents remain unknown. The FBI knew about Oswald and failed to act.

clue_marguerite_intelligence.title: Was Oswald a US intelligence operative?
clue_marguerite_intelligence.description: Oswald learned Russian at a CIA U-2 base, defected with apparent ease, was befriended by CIA-connected de Mohrenschildt upon return, and had his repatriation facilitated by the State Department. His mother insisted he was an agent. The pattern of intelligence connections is, at minimum, remarkable.

clue_paine_blanket.title: The blanket was empty — the rifle was gone
clue_paine_blanket.description: When police checked Ruth Paine's garage, the green and brown blanket that had held the Mannlicher-Carcano rifle was empty and limp. The rifle was gone. Oswald had visited the night before — his unusual midweek trip. The blanket's emptiness is physical proof that the rifle was removed from the garage on the night of November 21.

clue_greer_delay.title: Driver Greer turned to look back — twice
clue_greer_delay.description: The Zapruder film shows Secret Service driver William Greer turning to look back at the President TWICE after the first shot, instead of accelerating. The limousine nearly stopped. Standard Secret Service protocol demands immediate acceleration out of the kill zone. The fatal head shot struck during this 5-6 second delay. Had Greer followed protocol, the kill shot may never have been fired.
